name: Mike Gesicki
headline: Gesicki's 2025 collapse leaves no fantasy floor
body:
The 2024 version of Gesicki ranked 10th at the position on a 0.292 WOPR, 13.2% target share, and 83 targets that turned into 665 yards. None of that carried over. In 2025 he slid to TE36, drawing just 42 targets across 12 games for a 6.9% target share and a 0.175 target-per-route-run rate, while his route share cratered to 35.7% on a 38.5% snap rate. The 307 yards and two scores read like a backup line because that is what the role became.

The efficiency markers still flicker, a 0.127 EPA per play and 57.1% success rate, but volume that thin caps the ceiling. Cincinnati funnels everything to Chase, Higgins, and Chase Brown, with Fant and Sample splitting the leftovers behind him. He profiles as a touchdown-dependent dart in TE-premium and deep formats only, and a waiver name everywhere else until the snaps return.
